description: Replaced 5 non-circular `setXxxDeps` late-bound dependencies with direct ES module imports. Restructured 3 real cycles: extracted `read-button.js` (articles ↔ article-actions), extracted `feed-errors.js` (counts ↔ feeds), allowed safe circular import (articles ↔ pagination with hoisted functions). Removed all `setXxxDeps` functions except `setSidebarLoadCategory` (legitimate top-down wiring).
title: Phase 5: Eliminate late-bound dependencies

acceptance_criteria:
1. Users can create keyword and regex alert rules
2. New articles matching alert rules are automatically added to the alerts list at ingest time
3. Alerts page shows matched articles grouped by rule
4. Per-alert page allows editing/deleting rules and dismissing matches
5. Sidebar shows undismissed alert count badge
6. Articles matching both exclusion rules and alerts still appear in alerts
7. All new code passes make check
8. JS module has companion test file
description: Add a news alerts feature: if an article matches a user-defined keyword or regex, it gets added to a news alerts list. Alerts are global scope, match on title+summary at ingest time, and have their own dismissed/acknowledged state separate from read/unread. Articles matching both an exclusion rule and an alert still appear in the alerts list.
design:
## Data Model
- `news_alerts` table: id, user_id, name, pattern, is_regex, match_field (default 'title_summary'), created_at
- `article_alerts` join table: article_id, alert_id, matched_at, dismissed (bool)
- Unique constraint on (article_id, alert_id)

## Matching Engine
- New `srv/alerts.go` — loads user alert rules, pre-compiles regexes, matches title+summary
- Hooked into `OnFeedFetched` callback alongside existing `MarkExcludedArticlesReadForFeed`
- Alert evaluation runs independently of exclusion logic (alert wins if both match)
- No retroactive backfill — only newly fetched articles evaluated

## API
- CRUD on `/api/alerts` + dismiss endpoints on `/api/alerts/{id}/dismiss` and `/api/article-alerts/{id}/dismiss`
- Alert count added to `/api/counts` response

## UI
- Alerts list page (`GET /alerts`) grouped by rule, similar to history page
- Per-alert detail page (`GET /alerts/{id}`) for editing rule + viewing matches
- Create button on alerts list page
- Sidebar badge for undismissed alert count

## JS
- New `srv/static/modules/alerts.js` + companion test file
title: News Alerts

description:
Add migration 016-news-alerts.sql with:
- `news_alerts` table: id (PK), user_id (FK users, CASCADE), name (TEXT NOT NULL), pattern (TEXT NOT NULL), is_regex (INTEGER DEFAULT 0), match_field (TEXT DEFAULT 'title_summary'), created_at (TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
- `article_alerts` join table: id (PK), article_id (FK articles, CASCADE), alert_id (FK news_alerts, CASCADE), matched_at (TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP), dismissed (INTEGER DEFAULT 0)
- UNIQUE(article_id, alert_id) on article_alerts
- Indexes: news_alerts(user_id), article_alerts(alert_id), article_alerts(article_id)
title: Database schema for news alerts (migration 016)

description:
- New srv/static/modules/alerts.js — alerts page init, dismiss actions, create modal logic
- Companion srv/static/modules/alerts.test.js
- Wire into app.js for alerts page initialization
- Use data-action pattern for event delegation (consistent with other modules)
- API calls via api.js module
title: JS module for alerts (alerts.js + alerts.test.js)

description:
After all other news alerts tasks are complete, create a news alert rule with:
- Name: MA2
- Pattern: MA2
- is_regex: false
This serves as a smoke test that the full feature works end-to-end.
title: Create a news alert for the keyword MA2

description:
Add db/queries/alerts.sql with sqlc queries:
- CreateAlert, GetAlert, ListAlertsByUser, UpdateAlert, DeleteAlert
- InsertArticleAlert (ON CONFLICT IGNORE for idempotency)
- ListAlertArticlesGrouped (for alerts page — undismissed matches grouped by alert, with pagination)
- ListAlertArticles (for per-alert page — articles matching a specific alert)
- CountUndismissedAlerts (for sidebar badge)
- DismissArticleAlert (single match)
- DismissAllForAlert (all matches for one alert)
- ListUnreadArticlesByFeedForAlerts (get newly inserted articles to evaluate)

Run go generate ./db/... after adding queries.
title: sqlc queries for news alerts

description:
New file srv/alerts.go implementing alert evaluation:
- `EvaluateAlertsForFeed(ctx, feedID)` — looks up feed owner, loads user's alert rules, pre-compiles regex patterns, fetches unread articles for the feed, matches each against title+summary, inserts into article_alerts on match
- Pattern matching: plain keyword = case-insensitive substring match on title and summary; regex = compiled regexp match
- Pre-compile regexes once per evaluation batch (same pattern as shouldExclude in filter.go)
- Log matched alerts at info level for observability
- Handle errors gracefully (skip bad regexes, log warnings)
title: Alert matching engine (srv/alerts.go)

description:
In server.go, chain alert evaluation into the OnFeedFetched hook:
- Currently: srv.Fetcher.OnFeedFetched = srv.MarkExcludedArticlesReadForFeed
- Change to a wrapper that calls both MarkExcludedArticlesReadForFeed AND EvaluateAlertsForFeed
- Alert matching runs independently of exclusion logic (an excluded article can still trigger an alert)
- Order: exclusions first, then alerts (exclusions mark articles read, alerts don't care about read state)
title: Wire alert evaluation into ingest pipeline

description:
Add handlers in srv/server.go and register routes:
- POST /api/alerts — create alert (name, pattern, is_regex)
- GET /api/alerts — list user's alerts
- GET /api/alerts/{id} — get alert details + matched articles
- PUT /api/alerts/{id} — update alert (name, pattern, is_regex)
- DELETE /api/alerts/{id} — delete alert (cascades to article_alerts)
- POST /api/alerts/{id}/dismiss — dismiss all matches for an alert
- POST /api/article-alerts/{id}/dismiss — dismiss a single article-alert match

All endpoints scoped to authenticated user. Standard JSON request/response.
title: Alert API endpoints

description:
- Add alerts count to getArticleCounts() in server.go using CountUndismissedAlerts query
- Include 'alerts' field in /api/counts JSON response
- Update frontend counts.js to read the alerts count and render a badge on the alerts sidebar link
- Add alerts link to sidebar in base.html template
title: Add alert count to sidebar badge and counts API

description:
- New template srv/templates/alerts.html — similar layout to history.html
- Route: GET /alerts → handleAlerts
- Shows undismissed alert matches grouped by alert rule name/pattern
- Each group: rule name as section header, matched articles listed below
- Article rows show: title, feed name, matched_at timestamp, dismiss button
- 'Dismiss All' button per rule section
- 'Create Alert' button at top of page (links to create flow)
- Pagination within groups or flat with alert-name grouping
- Link each article title to /article/{id}

description:
- Route: GET /alerts/{id} → handleAlertDetail
- New template srv/templates/alert_detail.html
- Shows: alert name, pattern, is_regex status, created_at
- Edit form: update name, pattern, is_regex toggle — PUT /api/alerts/{id}

- Pagination for matched articles

description:
- 'Create Alert' button on the alerts list page
- Opens a modal or inline form with fields: name, pattern, is_regex checkbox
- Submits POST /api/alerts, refreshes page on success
- Validate: pattern is required, if is_regex validate regex compiles
- Style consistent with existing modals (feed edit modal pattern)

close_reason: Fixed in a034867: background goroutines use s.bgCtx, cancelled on server shutdown/test cleanup
description: Background goroutines in apiCreateFeed, apiRefreshFeed, and apiImportOPML used context.Background(), making them detached from the server lifecycle. During tests, these goroutines outlived the test, hit real network URLs (e.g. example.com), and logged WARN messages that caused transient test failures.
notes: Added bgCtx/bgCancel to Server struct. Background fetch goroutines now use s.bgCtx. testServer() cancels the context in t.Cleanup(). Suppressed log warnings on context cancellation.
title: Fix flaky Go test: background feed fetches outlive test lifecycle

close_reason: Added loggingMiddleware that logs every request with slog: method, path, status, duration, user_id, query. Uses Info/Warn/Error based on status code range. Skips static assets. Wired into handler chain.
description: No request logging exists. When something breaks in production there's nothing to look at — only scattered slog.Warn calls on specific errors. Add middleware that logs every request with slog: method, path, status code, duration, user ID. Use slog.Info for 2xx/3xx, slog.Warn for 4xx, slog.Error for 5xx. Keep it simple — no external dependencies.
title: Add structured request logging middleware

description: The database (25k+ articles of curated content) has no backup strategy. A bad migration, disk failure, or accidental deletion loses everything. Add a daily backup using SQLite's .backup command (safe with WAL mode). Options: a systemd timer running 'sqlite3 db.sqlite3 ".backup db-backup.sqlite3"', or a rotating scheme that keeps the last N backups. Keep it simple — local file backups are fine for a self-hosted app.
title: Add automated SQLite database backups

acceptance_criteria: Perf test baselines remain at or below current thresholds. Favicon proxy uses an in-memory cache. Unread counts are cached and invalidated on writes. Article list queries use covering indexes for common access patterns. No N+1 queries in hot paths.
description: Optimize response times, reduce redundant work, and improve caching across the app. The app already has gzip compression, static file cache-busting, and perf regression tests. This epic targets the remaining low-hanging fruit: server-side caching for expensive computations, smarter HTTP caching, database query optimization, and reducing redundant upstream requests.
title: Performance and caching improvements

close_reason: Won't fix — browser already caches favicons via Cache-Control header. Server-side LRU adds complexity for negligible benefit on a self-hosted app.
description: Every sidebar render triggers /api/favicon?domain=... requests that proxy through to Google S2. With 25k+ articles and many feeds, these are repeated constantly. Add a bounded in-memory cache (e.g. sync.Map or LRU) keyed by domain that stores the favicon bytes/content-type. TTL of ~7 days matches the current Cache-Control header. This eliminates redundant upstream requests and speeds up page loads.
title: Add in-memory cache for favicon proxy

description: The /api/counts endpoint runs multiple COUNT(*) queries on every poll (articles, feeds, categories). These are hit frequently by the JS frontend to update sidebar badges. Add a per-user in-memory cache for count results that is invalidated when articles are marked read/unread/starred or new articles are fetched. This turns the most frequent API call into a cache hit.
title: Cache unread/starred counts with write-through invalidation

close_reason: Won't fix — premature optimization. SQLite handles 25k articles with single-column indexes in ~5-30ms per endpoint. Composite indexes not needed at this scale.
description: Article list queries ORDER BY COALESCE(published_at, fetched_at) DESC with filters on feed_id, is_read, user_id via JOIN. Current indexes are single-column (feed_id, published_at, is_read separately). Add composite indexes that cover the common query patterns: (feed_id, published_at DESC), (is_read, published_at DESC) for the main feed. Run EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN on the hot queries before and after to verify index usage. With 25k articles this will matter.
title: Add composite indexes for article list queries

description: HTML page responses set no ETag or Last-Modified headers. The browser must always download the full response. Generate a weak ETag based on a hash of the rendered output (or a version counter tied to data mutations) and return 304 Not Modified when the client sends a matching If-None-Match. This saves bandwidth and speeds up back/forward navigation. Start with the article detail page (content rarely changes) and the main index.
title: Add ETag/conditional responses for HTML pages

description: The feeds page and sidebar compute unread counts. Check whether per-feed unread counts are fetched in a single batch query (GetUnreadCountsByFeed) or individually per feed. The existing GetUnreadCountsByFeed and GetUnreadCountsByCategory queries look correct, but verify the handler code paths in handleIndex, handleFeeds, and apiGetCounts don't issue additional per-feed queries. The perf_test.go seeds 200 feeds — ensure no regression.
title: Optimize feed list queries to avoid N+1 for unread counts

description: The existing perf_test.go covers endpoint latency but not individual query performance. Add Go benchmarks (func BenchmarkXxx) for the hottest queries: unread article listing, count queries, category article listing, and search. This provides a baseline for measuring index and query optimizations and catches regressions at the query level rather than the HTTP level.
title: Add benchmark tests for database queries

description:
Extracted 17 stateful modules from app.js monolith: settings, dropdown, timestamps, views, sidebar, articles, article-actions, pagination, feeds, folders, counts, drag-drop, opml, queue, settings-page, offline, scraper-page. Each module has a companion .test.js file. Used late-bound `setXxxDeps()` pattern for circular dependencies. Wired transitional `window.X` exports for inline onclick handlers.
Reduced app.js from 2124 to 386 lines.
title: Phase 2: Extract stateful modules

close_reason: Added toast notification module (toast.js) with error/success/info types, animated slide-up toasts with auto-dismiss. Replaced all alert() calls and added showToast() alongside all silent console.error() calls across 12 modules. Updated all affected tests. All 433 tests pass.
description: Currently folders.js uses alert() on failure while feeds.js, article-actions.js, counts.js, drag-drop.js and others silently console.error(). The user sees nothing when a star toggle, feed load, or drag-drop reorder fails. Add a toast/notification system or at minimum surface errors visibly across all modules.
title: Consistent user-facing error handling

description: Replaced all 89 inline `onclick`/`onchange`/`oninput`/`onsubmit` handlers across 7 template files with `data-action` attributes and delegated `addEventListener`. Also replaced onclick strings built in JS modules. Removed all `window.X` transitional exports and ESLint `varsIgnorePattern` whitelist.
title: Phase 3: Eliminate inline event handlers

description: Created `srv/static/modules/` directory. Extracted pure utility modules first: `icons.js` (SVG constants), `utils.js` (formatTimeAgo, stripHtml, etc.), `api.js` (fetch wrapper). Added direct-import tests for utils.js and api.js. Updated vitest coverage config.
title: Phase 1: Infrastructure (non-breaking)

acceptance_criteria: All child tasks completed. No known high-severity vulnerabilities remain. Security headers present. Content sanitization in place. Rate limiting on sensitive endpoints.
description: Comprehensive security review of the feedreader application. Audit for common web vulnerabilities (XSS, SSRF, injection, missing headers, rate limiting, etc.) and apply best practices.
title: Security audit and hardening

close_reason: Added bluemonday HTML sanitizer. The safeHTML template function now runs content through a UGC-based policy that strips scripts, event handlers, javascript: URLs, and form elements while preserving formatting, images, iframes, and media. Tests added in sanitize_test.go.
description: Article content from RSS feeds is rendered via safeHTML template function which bypasses Go's html/template escaping. This means any JavaScript in feed content (e.g. <script>, onerror=, javascript: URLs) is rendered directly into the page. Need to add an HTML sanitizer (e.g. bluemonday) that strips dangerous tags/attributes while preserving safe formatting.
title: Sanitize RSS/Atom content before rendering (XSS)

close_reason: Added securityHeaders middleware setting X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, and a comprehensive CSP. CSP allows unsafe-inline for scripts/styles (required by existing inline manifest generator and settings injection), HTTPS sources for images/media/frames (for feed content), blob: for manifest, and blocks object embeds. Tests added in security_headers_test.go.
description: No security headers are set on responses. Add middleware that sets: Content-Security-Policy (restrict script/style sources), X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin. Consider CSP carefully given the use of inline scripts and safeHTML content.
title: Add security response headers

description: No rate limiting exists on any endpoint. Add per-user rate limiting on write endpoints (feed creation, refresh, scraper generation, AI endpoints). This prevents abuse and protects the Anthropic API key from excessive usage. Consider using golang.org/x/time/rate or a simple token bucket.
title: Add rate limiting to API endpoints

description: No MaxBytesReader or body size limits on incoming requests. Large POST bodies could cause memory exhaustion. Add http.MaxBytesReader on all endpoints that accept request bodies (API JSON endpoints, OPML import, etc.). Also set ReadTimeout/WriteTimeout/MaxHeaderBytes on the http.Server.
title: Add request body size limits

close_reason: Added safenet package with SSRF protection: blocks private/loopback/link-local IPs, validates schemes, checks resolved IPs at dial time, limits redirects. Applied to feed fetcher, favicon proxy, and Steam API client. Added URL validation in feed create/update endpoints.
description: Users provide arbitrary URLs for feed subscriptions and scraper targets. These are fetched server-side, creating SSRF risk. The Steam API endpoint in server.go also uses user-supplied appID in http.Get. Audit all outbound HTTP requests and add: URL validation (block private IPs/localhost/metadata endpoints), timeouts on all HTTP clients, and redirect-following limits.
title: Audit SSRF risk in feed fetcher and scraper

description: No CSRF tokens or SameSite cookie protections are used. While auth is header-based (X-Exedev-Userid from proxy), state-changing POST/PUT/DELETE endpoints should verify requests originate from the app. Options: check Origin/Referer headers, require a custom header (X-Requested-With), or add CSRF tokens. Since the app uses fetch() for API calls, requiring a custom header is the simplest approach.
title: Add CSRF protection for state-changing endpoints

description: Multiple JS modules use innerHTML/outerHTML with content that may include unsanitized data from the API (article titles, content, feed names). While server-side sanitization (if added) helps, client-side code should also be defensive. Audit all innerHTML assignments in modules/*.js and ensure user-controlled strings are escaped before insertion into HTML strings.
title: Audit innerHTML usage in client-side JS

description: The http.Server has no ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, IdleTimeout, or MaxHeaderBytes configured. This makes the server vulnerable to slowloris and similar denial-of-service attacks. Add sensible defaults: ReadTimeout=15s, WriteTimeout=60s (longer for SSE/streaming if any), IdleTimeout=120s, MaxHeaderBytes=1MB.
title: Set HTTP server timeouts

acceptance_criteria: All interactive elements keyboard-accessible. Focus visible on all focusable elements. Landmarks and ARIA roles in place. Skip-to-content link present. Modals and dropdowns trap focus. axe-core audit shows no critical or serious violations on main pages.
description: The app has minimal accessibility support. Only one aria-label exists (menu toggle button). Focus outlines are suppressed on inputs. No keyboard navigation for dropdowns, modals, or the sidebar. No skip-to-content link. No ARIA roles or live regions (except toasts). Screen reader users cannot effectively use the app.
title: Accessibility improvements

close_reason: Replaced all 4 instances of outline:none in style.css with :focus-visible styles (2px solid accent outline with 2px offset). Added a global :focus-visible rule for all interactive elements. Keyboard users now see clear focus indicators while mouse users get clean interactions.
description: 4 instances of 'outline: none' in style.css suppress focus indicators on search inputs, form fields, and selects. Keyboard users cannot see which element is focused. Replace with :focus-visible styles that show a visible outline/ring for keyboard navigation while keeping mouse interactions clean. Use something like 'outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 2px' on :focus-visible.
title: Fix focus visibility — replace outline:none with proper focus styles

description: base.html has <aside>, <nav>, and <main> elements which is good, but there's no skip-to-content link for keyboard users to bypass the sidebar navigation. Add a visually-hidden skip link as the first focusable element that jumps to main content. Also add role=banner to the header area and aria-label attributes to distinguish the main nav from any secondary navigation.
title: Add skip-to-content link and landmark roles

description: Dropdowns (dropdown.js) and modals (new folder modal in folders.js, feed edit modal) have no keyboard support. Needed: Escape to close, arrow keys to navigate dropdown items, Tab trapping within open modals, Enter/Space to activate items. The dropdown toggle should set aria-expanded. Dropdown menus need role=menu and items need role=menuitem.
title: Add keyboard navigation for dropdowns and modals

description: Article lists are loaded dynamically via JS (innerHTML in articles.js). These need: aria-live=polite on the article list container so screen readers announce new content, aria-busy=true while loading, meaningful aria-labels on action buttons (star, queue, mark read — currently just SVG icons with no text alternative). The unread count badges in the sidebar should use aria-label to convey counts.
title: Add ARIA attributes to dynamic content

close_reason: Added aria-label to all icon-only buttons: folder chevron, folder settings link, delete rule, rename/delete/edit/refresh/close buttons in feeds, more-actions dropdown, schema help/close in scrapers, copy newsletter in settings. Also added aria-label to JS-rendered buttons (read, star, queue, external link) in articles.js, and dynamic aria-label updates in article-actions.js and read-button.js when toggling state.
description: Many buttons across templates contain only SVG icons with no accessible text: folder settings gear, rename pencil, delete trash, star toggle, queue toggle, mark-read buttons, drag handles, chevron toggles. Each needs an aria-label describing its action. The menu toggle button already has aria-label — use that as the pattern. Also audit JS-rendered buttons in articles.js and article-actions.js.
title: Add accessible labels to icon-only buttons

description: The app uses CSS custom properties for colors (--text-secondary, --text-primary, --bg-primary, etc.) but contrast ratios haven't been verified. --text-secondary on --bg-primary is the most likely failure since secondary text is intentionally lighter. Check both light and dark themes. Use a contrast checker tool and adjust colors to meet WCAG AA (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text).
title: Ensure color contrast meets WCAG AA

description: Moved page-specific logic out of app.js: add feed form (72 lines) to feeds.js, search handler (36 lines) to new search.js, sidebar mobile close to sidebar.js, pagination bootstrap to pagination.js, queue hydration to article-actions.js, drag prevention to drag-drop.js. Added `<link rel="modulepreload">` hints for all 23 modules. Final app.js: 126 lines (imports, init calls, DOMContentLoaded sequencing).
title: Phase 6: Clean up app.js entry point

acceptance_criteria:
- app.js is a thin entry point (~120 lines): imports, init calls, DOMContentLoaded sequencing
- All JS logic lives in ES modules under srv/static/modules/
- Every module has a companion .test.js with meaningful unit tests
- Zero inline event handlers (onclick/onchange/etc) in templates or JS-built HTML
- Zero window.X global exports
- Import maps with content hashes for cache busting
- No setXxxDeps late-binding hacks (except setSidebarLoadCategory for legitimate top-down wiring)
- make check passes clean
- Coverage: >80% statements, >85% functions on modules
description:
Refactor `srv/static/app.js` (2277 lines, plain `<script>`) into ES modules.
**Goals:**
1. Coverage — V8 coverage works with real ES module imports
2. Testability — direct imports replace the `new Function()` eval hack
3. Maintainability — split monolith into focused, navigable modules
4. Tooling — static analysis, go-to-definition, dead code detection
**Final state:**
- 23 ES modules in `srv/static/modules/`, each with a companion `.test.js`
- 425 tests across 23 test files
- `app.js` is a 126-line entry point (down from 2277 original)
- Zero inline event handlers — all use `data-action` + `addEventListener`
- Zero `window.X` global exports
- Import maps provide cache busting for all module files

See `docs/ES_MODULES_PLAN.md` for full design rationale.
See `docs/BENCHMARK_BASELINE.md` for performance measurements.
design:
# ES Modules Refactor Plan

Refactor `srv/static/app.js` (2277 lines, plain `<script>`) into ES modules.

## Goals

1. **Coverage** — V8 coverage works with real ES module imports
2. **Testability** — direct imports replace the `new Function()` eval hack
3. **Maintainability** — split monolith into focused, navigable modules
4. **Tooling** — static analysis, go-to-definition, dead code detection

## Current State (after Phase 4)

- `app.js` is a 363-line entry point loaded as `<script type="module">`
- 20 ES modules in `srv/static/modules/`, each with a companion `.test.js`
- 390 tests across 20 test files, all using direct ES module imports
- Zero inline event handlers — all use `data-action` + `addEventListener`
- Zero `window.X` global exports
- Import maps provide cache busting for all module files
- 82% statement / 85% function coverage on modules
- 1 `setSidebarLoadCategory()` late-binding call remains (legitimate top-down wiring)
- 2 shared leaf modules (`feed-errors.js`, `read-button.js`) break former cycles
- 1 safe circular ES module import (`articles ↔ pagination`) with hoisted functions

## Proposed Module Structure

```
srv/static/
  app.js              → thin entry point (imports modules, runs init)
  modules/
    api.js            → api() fetch wrapper
    settings.js       → getSetting, saveSetting, applyUserPreferences,
                        applyHideReadArticles, applyHideEmptyFeeds
    utils.js          → formatTimeAgo, formatLocalDate, stripHtml,
                        truncateText, getArticleSortTime
    icons.js          → SVG constant strings
    articles.js       → renderArticleActions, buildArticleCardHtml,
                        renderArticles, updateReadButton,
                        showArticlesLoading, updateAllReadMessage,
                        showReadArticles, showHiddenArticles,
                        processEmbeds, extractYouTubeId
    article-actions.js→ markRead, markUnread, toggleStar, toggleQueue,
                        markReadSilent, openArticle, openArticleExternal,
                        markAsRead, auto-mark-read observer
    pagination.js     → cursor state, loadMoreArticles, checkScrollForMore,
                        updatePaginationCursor, updateEndOfArticlesIndicator
    sidebar.js        → toggleSidebar, setSidebarActive, navigateFolder,
                        toggleFolderCollapse, collapseFolder
    feeds.js          → loadFeedArticles, loadCategoryArticles,
                        refreshFeed, deleteFeed, editFeed, saveFeed,
                        filterFeeds, setFeedCategory, feed error banner,
                        createEditFeedModal, closeEditModal
    folders.js        → openCreateFolderModal, closeCreateFolderModal,
                        submitCreateFolder, renameCategory,
                        unparentCategory, deleteCategory
    counts.js         → updateCounts, updateFeedStatusCell,
                        updateFeedErrors
    views.js          → setView, getViewScope, initView,
                        migrateLegacyViewDefaults, getDefaultViewForScope,
                        applyDefaultViewForScope
    drag-drop.js      → initDragDrop, initFolderDragDrop,
                        syncFolderOrder, reorderElements,
                        getDragAfterElementAmongSiblings
    opml.js           → exportOPML, importOPML
    queue.js          → queuedArticleIds, queuedIdsReady, queueNext
    settings-page.js  → initSettingsPage, runCleanup, newsletter functions
    offline.js        → service worker, online/offline handling,
                        cacheQueueForOffline, replayPendingActions
    dropdown.js       → toggleDropdown, click-outside listener
    timestamps.js     → initTimestampTooltips
    scraper-page.js   → scraper tab switching, schema panel, config modal
                        (currently inline in scrapers.html <script>)
```

## Migration Strategy

### Phase 1: Infrastructure (non-breaking)

1. Create `modules/` directory
2. Extract pure utility modules first (`utils.js`, `icons.js`, `api.js`)
   — these have zero DOM dependencies and are easiest to test
3. Update `vitest.config.mjs` to include `modules/` in coverage
4. Write module-style tests that directly import from the new files
5. Verify old tests still pass (both test styles coexist temporarily)

### Phase 2: Extract stateful modules

6. Extract `settings.js`, `views.js`, `dropdown.js`, `timestamps.js`
7. Extract `articles.js`, `article-actions.js`, `pagination.js`
8. Extract `sidebar.js`, `feeds.js`, `folders.js`, `counts.js`
9. Extract `drag-drop.js`, `opml.js`, `queue.js`
10. Extract `settings-page.js`, `offline.js`

During this phase, `app.js` gradually shrinks as code moves out. Each
extracted module exports its public functions. The entry point imports
and either calls them or registers them.

### Phase 3: Eliminate inline handlers

11. Replace `onclick="fn()"` in templates with `data-action` attributes
    or stable selectors, and wire them up via `addEventListener` in the
    entry point or per-page init functions
12. Replace `onclick` strings built in JS (article cards, modals) with
    DOM creation + `addEventListener`
13. Remove the ESLint `varsIgnorePattern` whitelist
14. Remove all `window.X = X` assignments (nothing needs to be global)

### Phase 4: Finalize

15. Change `<script src="/static/app.js">` to
    `<script type="module" src="/static/app.js">` in `base.html`
16. Delete `test-helper.js` and the eval-based test machinery
17. Rewrite tests as direct ES module imports
18. Verify coverage reports meaningful numbers
19. Update `AGENTS.md` code layout docs

### Phase 5: Eliminate late-bound dependencies

The `setXxxDeps()` pattern was used during Phase 2 to break circular
import dependencies. Of the 6 uses, only 3 are actual cycles — the
other 5 are unnecessary and can be replaced with direct imports. The
3 real cycles can be eliminated through small restructurings.

**Non-circular (replace with direct imports):**

- `article-actions` → `counts.updateCounts` — no cycle
- `article-actions` → `offline.updateQueueCacheIfStandalone` — no cycle
- `counts` → `articles.applyUserPreferences` — no cycle
- `offline` → `counts.updateCounts` — no cycle
- `queue` → `offline.updateQueueCacheIfStandalone` — no cycle

**Real cycles (restructure to eliminate):**

20. `article-actions ↔ articles`: `article-actions` needs
    `updateReadButton` from `articles`, and `articles` imports
    `queuedArticleIds`, `queuedIdsReady`, `initAutoMarkRead` from
    `article-actions`. Fix: extract `updateReadButton` into a small
    shared module (e.g. `read-button.js`) — it's a pure DOM
    manipulation function that updates a button's icon and data
    attributes. Both modules import from it.

21. `articles ↔ pagination`: `pagination` imports
    `buildArticleCardHtml`, `processEmbeds`, `applyUserPreferences`,
    `getShowingHiddenArticles` from `articles`, while `articles`
    needs `updatePaginationCursor`, `updateEndOfArticlesIndicator`,
    `setPaginationState` from `pagination`. Fix: the pagination state
    functions that `articles` needs are really part of "rendering
    articles" — move them into `articles.js`, making `pagination.js`
    a pure "load more" module that calls into `articles`.
    Alternatively, extract the shared rendering functions
    (`buildArticleCardHtml`, `processEmbeds`) into a lower-level
    `article-rendering.js`.

22. `counts ↔ feeds` and `sidebar ↔ feeds`: `feeds` imports
    `updateCounts` from `counts` and `setSidebarActive` from
    `sidebar`. `counts` needs `showFeedErrorBanner` /
    `removeFeedErrorBanner` from `feeds`. `sidebar` needs
    `loadCategoryArticles` from `feeds`. Fix: move
    `showFeedErrorBanner`/`removeFeedErrorBanner` into `counts.js`
    (or a new `feed-errors.js`) since they're only called from
    `updateFeedErrors` in counts. For sidebar → feeds,
    `loadCategoryArticles` is a high-level orchestration function —
    keep the callback from `app.js` (this is legitimate top-down
    wiring, not a circular dependency hack).

After this phase, the only remaining `setXxxDeps` call should be
`setSidebarLoadCategory` in `app.js`, which is genuine top-down
wiring from the entry point.

### Phase 6: Clean up app.js entry point

`app.js` still contains ~160 lines of page-specific logic that
belongs in modules. The entry point should only import, wire
dependencies, and call init functions.

**Move to existing modules:**

23. Add feed form handler (72 lines) → `feeds.js` as
    `initAddFeedForm()`. Contains Reddit URL building, HuggingFace
    config construction, category assignment — all feed-specific
    logic with no entry-point concerns.

24. Feed item click handler (12 lines) → `feeds.js` as part of
    `initFeedActionListeners()`. SPA feed navigation that already
    calls `loadFeedArticles`.

25. Sidebar mobile close (8 lines) → `sidebar.js` as part of
    `initSidebarListeners()`. Sidebar behavior.

26. Pagination bootstrap + scroll listener (11 lines) →
    `pagination.js` as `initPagination()`. Reads DOM for initial
    cursor state and registers the scroll listener — pure pagination
    concerns.

27. Queue hydration (16 lines) → `article-actions.js` as
    `initQueueState()`. Fetches queue IDs from the API, populates
    `queuedArticleIds`, and renders action-button placeholders.
    Already owns that state.

28. Drag prevention (4 lines) → `drag-drop.js` as part of its init.

**Move to new module:**

29. Search handler (36 lines) → new `search.js` module. Self-contained
    feature with its own state (`originalHTML`, `searchAbort`,
    debounce timer) and no dependency on other app.js code.

**Keep in app.js:**

- Imports (~25 lines after cleanup)
- Listener init calls (~16 lines)
- Expand active folder parents (8 lines) — cross-cutting DOM init
- One-liner init calls: `initView`, `initFolderDragDrop`, page inits
- `setInterval(updateCounts, 60000)` — app-level polling policy
- `setSidebarLoadCategory` wiring (legitimate top-down callback)

After this phase, `app.js` should be ~120 lines — almost entirely
imports, init calls, and DOMContentLoaded sequencing.

Phase 6 also adds `<link rel="modulepreload">` hints in `base.html`
for all 20 modules. The ES module import waterfall added +328ms to
cold DOMContentLoaded (the browser discovers imports sequentially
across 3 depth levels). Modulepreload lets the browser fetch all
modules in parallel upfront, eliminating the waterfall.

## Key Decisions

### No bundler

Modern browsers handle ES module imports natively. This is a single-user
app behind a proxy — the extra HTTP requests for ~15 module files are
irrelevant. A bundler adds build complexity for no real benefit here.

### Transitional `window` exports

During Phases 2–3, functions still called from `onclick` attributes must
remain on `window`. The entry point can do:
```js
import { toggleStar } from './modules/article-actions.js';
window.toggleStar = toggleStar;
```
This is removed in Phase 3 when inline handlers are eliminated.

### Shared state

Mutable state (`paginationCursorTime`, `queuedArticleIds`, etc.) lives
in the module that owns it, exposed via getter/setter functions rather
than direct variable access. This makes dependencies explicit.

### Test migration

Tests can be migrated incrementally. As each module is extracted, its
tests switch from `window.fn()` to `import { fn }`. The eval-based
test helper remains functional for not-yet-extracted code.

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Circular dependencies between modules | 3 real cycles identified (see Phase 5); resolved via small restructurings — extract shared leaf modules or consolidate tightly-coupled state |
| Breaking inline handlers mid-migration | Transitional `window` exports keep everything working |
| `<script type="module">` is deferred (runs later than classic scripts) | This app already uses `DOMContentLoaded`; deferred execution is equivalent |
| `type="module"` doesn't run in very old browsers | Not a concern — single-user app on modern browser |
| Large diff is hard to review | Phase-by-phase commits, each independently passing `make check` |

# Page Load Benchmark — Baseline

Captured **2026-02-24** before ES modules refactor.
Single `<script>` tag loading `app.js` (87 KB raw, 26 KB gzipped).

## Navigation Timing

| Metric              | Time   |
|---------------------|--------|
| TTFB                | 124ms  |
| DOM Interactive      | 218ms  |
| DOMContentLoaded     | 226ms  |
| Load Complete        | 231ms  |
| First Paint          | 236ms  |
| First Contentful Paint | 236ms |

## JS/CSS Assets

| Asset     | Raw Size | Transfer (gzip) | Duration |
|-----------|----------|-----------------|----------|
| app.js    | 87 KB    | 26 KB           | 18ms     |
| style.css | 53 KB    | 13 KB           | 11ms     |
| script.js | 547 B    | —               | —        |

## Full Page Summary

| Metric          | Value      |
|-----------------|------------|
| Total requests  | 35         |
| Total transfer  | 185 KB     |
| JS requests     | 1 (app.js) |
| Favicon requests | 33        |

## Notes

- Measured via browser Performance API through mitmdump reverse proxy
  (localhost:3000 → localhost:8000)
- 33 of 35 requests are sidebar favicon images (~1 KB each, 82–224ms each
  due to DB lookups / proxying)
- One fetch request to `/api/queue` (120 KB, 92ms) fires after DOMContentLoaded
- JS/CSS load times are negligible (<20ms) since it's all localhost
- After the refactor, expect ~15 JS module requests replacing the single
  app.js request. Each module will be 1–10 KB. Impact on load time should
  be minimal given localhost latency.

## How to Re-measure

```bash
# Start auth proxy if not running
mitmdump --mode reverse:http://localhost:8000 --listen-port 3000 \
  --set modify_headers='/~q/X-Exedev-Email/test@example.com' \
  --set modify_headers='/~q/X-Exedev-Userid/testuser123'
```

Then in the browser console on `http://localhost:3000/`:

```js
const nav = performance.getEntriesByType('navigation')[0];
const paint = performance.getEntriesByType('paint');
const resources = performance.getEntriesByType('resource');
console.table({
  ttfb: Math.round(nav.responseStart),
  domInteractive: Math.round(nav.domInteractive),
  domContentLoaded: Math.round(nav.domContentLoadedEventEnd),
  loadComplete: Math.round(nav.loadEventEnd),
});
console.log('Paint:', paint.map(p => `${p.name}: ${Math.round(p.startTime)}ms`));
console.log(`${resources.length} requests, ${Math.round(resources.reduce((s,r) => s + (r.transferSize||0), 0) / 1024)} KB`);
```

# Page Load Benchmark — After ES Modules Refactor

Captured **2026-02-25** after completing Phases 1–4 of the ES modules
refactor. `app.js` is now a 363-line entry point loading 20 ES modules
via `<script type="module">` with import maps for cache busting.

## Navigation Timing (cold load)

| Metric               | Time   | Δ from baseline |
|----------------------|--------|----------------|
| TTFB                 | 257ms  | +133ms         |
| DOM Interactive      | 378ms  | +160ms         |
| DOMContentLoaded     | 554ms  | +328ms         |
| Load Complete        | 570ms  | +339ms         |
| First Paint          | 404ms  | +168ms         |
| First Contentful Paint | 404ms | +168ms         |

## Navigation Timing (warm/cached load)

| Metric               | Time       |
|----------------------|------------|
| TTFB                 | 223–264ms  |
| DOMContentLoaded     | 287–331ms  |
| Load Complete        | 293–336ms  |
| First Paint          | 292–336ms  |
| Transfer             | **0 KB**   |

## JS/CSS Assets

| Asset                  | Raw Size | Transfer (gzip) |
|------------------------|----------|----------------|
| app.js                 | 13 KB    | 4 KB           |
| modules/feeds.js       | 17 KB    | 4 KB           |
| modules/articles.js    | 13 KB    | 4 KB           |
| modules/article-actions.js | 12 KB | 3 KB          |
| modules/scraper-page.js | 11 KB   | 3 KB           |
| modules/drag-drop.js   | 9 KB     | 2 KB           |
| modules/offline.js     | 8 KB     | 3 KB           |
| 14 smaller modules     | 36 KB    | 12 KB          |
| **Total JS**           | **119 KB** | **35 KB**    |
| style.css              | 52 KB    | 13 KB          |

## Full Page Summary

| Metric           | Value          | Δ from baseline |
|------------------|----------------|----------------|
| Total requests   | 58             | +23            |
| JS requests      | 21             | +20            |
| JS raw size      | 119 KB         | +32 KB (+37%)  |
| JS gzip size     | 35 KB          | +9 KB (+35%)   |
| Favicon requests | 35             | +2             |

## Analysis

**Cold load is slower** due to the ES module import waterfall. The
browser discovers imports sequentially as it parses each module,
creating a dependency chain. With 20 modules and a 3-level import
depth, this adds multiple sequential round trips even on localhost.

**Warm load is fast** — import maps with content hashes enable
immutable caching (`max-age=31536000`), so subsequent visits transfer
0 bytes and load in ~300ms, comparable to the pre-refactor cold load.

**Raw JS size grew 37%** — import/export statements and late-binding
boilerplate (`setXxxDeps` patterns) add overhead. Gzip is also less
effective across many small files vs. one large file.

## Potential Optimizations

- **`<link rel="modulepreload">`** for each module in `base.html`
  would eliminate the waterfall by fetching all modules in parallel
- **Phase 5/6 cleanup** will reduce module count and remove
  late-binding boilerplate
- **Bundling** for production remains an option but was explicitly
  deferred (see ES_MODULES_PLAN.md)
